zh.Lot-et-Garonne, Aquitaine, France: 法國努維勒-阿吉泰洛吉吉加洛
zh.lifeSummary: 當Rotrou II du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun於1021年出生在法國艾-吉洛易a奧度時，他的父親Geoffroy Ier du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun34歲，母親Helvise de Corbon22歲。他於大約1040年與Comtesse Adeline de Domfront de Montagne結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1079年3月1日在法國崔艾-吉洛易a奧度享年58歲。

zh-hans.Lot-et-Garonne, Aquitaine, France: 法国努維勒-阿吉泰洛吉吉加洛
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Rotrou II du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun于1021年出生在法国艾-吉洛易a奧度时，他的父亲Geoffroy Ier du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun34岁，母亲Helvise de Corbon22岁。他于大约1040年与Comtesse Adeline de Domfront de Montagne结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1079年3月1日在法国崔艾-吉洛易a奧度享年58岁。

nameMeanings.name: du
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Perche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Mexico): unexplained.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Rotrou II du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun was born in 1021, in Châteaudun, Eure-et-Loir, France, his father, Geoffroy Ier du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun, was 34 and his mother, Helvise de Corbon, was 22. He married Comtesse Adeline de Domfront de Montagne about 1040. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 1 March 1079, in Châteaudun, Eure-et-Loir, Centre, France, at the age of 58.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Rotrou II du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun est né en 1021, à Châteaudun, Eure-et-Loir, France, son père, Geoffroy Ier du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Helvise de Corbon, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Comtesse Adeline de Domfront de Montagne environ 1040. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 1 mars 1079, à Châteaudun, Eure-et-Loir, Centre, France, à l’âge de 58 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Rotrou II du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun 1021 in Châteaudun, Eure-et-Loir, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Geoffroy Ier du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun 34 und seine Mutter Helvise de Corbon 22. Er hat um 1040 Comtesse Adeline de Domfront de Montagne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 1. März 1079 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Châteaudun, Eure-et-Loir, Centre, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Rotrou II du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun è nato il nel 1021 nel Châteaudun, Eure-et-Loir, Francia, il suo padre, Geoffroy Ier du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Helvise de Corbon, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Comtesse Adeline de Domfront de Montagne circa 1040. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 1 marzo 1079, nel Châteaudun, Eure-et-Loir, Centre, Francia, all'età di 58 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Rotrou II du Perche vicomte de Châteaudunが1021年にフランスエウレ-エト-ロイルチaテアウダンで生まれた時，父Geoffroy Ier du Perche vicomte de Châteaudunは34歳，母Helvise de Corbonは22歳であった。約1040年にComtesse Adeline de Domfront de Montagneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1079年3月1日にフランスセントアエウレ-エト-ロイルチaテアウダンで58歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Rotrou II du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun(이)가 1021년 프랑스 우레-트-로이르 cha테오던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Geoffroy Ier du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Helvise de Corbon(은)는 22세였다. 그는 대략 1040년 Comtesse Adeline de Domfront de Montagne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1079년 3월 1일 프랑스 세트르 우레-트-로이르 cha테오던에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Rotrou II du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun nasceu em 1021, em Châteaudun, Eure-et-Loir, França, seu pai, Geoffroy Ier du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Helvise de Corbon, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Comtesse Adeline de Domfront de Montagne aproximadamente 1040. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1 de março de 1079, em Châteaudun, Eure-et-Loir, Centre, França, com 58 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rotrou II du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun родился в 1021 г. в местности чaтодун, эуре-ет-лоир, Франция, его отцу по имени Geoffroy Ier du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Helvise de Corbon, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Comtesse Adeline de Domfront de Montagne около 1040 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 1 марта 1079 г. в местности чaтодун, эуре-ет-лоир, кентр, Франция в возрасте 58 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Rotrou II du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun nació en 1021, en Châteaudun, Eure-et-Loir, Francia, su papá, Geoffroy Ier du Perche vicomte de Châteaudun, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Helvise de Corbon, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Comtesse Adeline de Domfront de Montagne aproximadamente 1040. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 1 de marzo de 1079, en Châteaudun, Eure-et-Loir, Centre, Francia, a la edad de 58 años.
